title: <x-subst type="function">
breadcrumbs: XHTML Elements for Static Server Pages
text: <x-subst type="function"> XHTML placeholder that is replaced by the value of the specified ProcScript function; one of $usp_formtitle , $putmess , or $text . Explain this code <x-substtype="function" name="AllowedProcScriptFunction">Content</x-subst> Copy Example: <x-subst type = "function" name =" $text ( IDString )"> $text ( IDString ) </x-subst> Arguments AllowedProcScriptFunction —name of the function containing the value to be substituted; can be: $usp_formtitle —sets the server page title using the value of $formtitle . $putmess —display the contents of the message frame . $text —display labels with $text . IDString —ID of the label text to be substituted. $usp_formtitle The layout of a static server page typically contains the following element specifying the initial server page title: <x-subst type="function" name="$usp_formtitle"> <title>$usp_formtitle </title> </x-subst> Copy When webgen parses the layout, it replaces the <x-subst> tags by: <title> ServerPageTitle </title> Copy where ServerPageTitle is the current value of $formtitle . This title is displayed in the browser tab displaying the USP. $text When using function substitution with $text , the current value of the function $text ( IDString ) is substituted for labels with " $text ( IDString ) " as their contents. Related Links Substituting ProcScript Functions and Including Files in Static Server Pages You can use the <x-subst> tag to substitute some ProcScript functions ($formtitle, $putmess and $text) or include files on a static server page. $text Return the text of a message or help text. $putmess Send a message to the message frame, or return the contents of the message frame. webgen Generate an XHTML document from the layout definition and the component definition of a static server page.
